summary: Verified Hard Reset bug fix. Backend endpoint POST /api/database/hard-reset now uses Pydantic HardResetRequest model. Frontend properly handles Pydantic validation array errors and 401 responses. Modal text updated to clarify it's the CURRENT admin password for validation, not a new one.
passed: Login as admin/Admin3b4e892c returns 200 with token
POST /api/database/hard-reset with wrong password returns 401 with detail:'Contraseña incorrecta' (string, not object)
POST /api/database/hard-reset with missing body returns 422 with Pydantic array (frontend handles gracefully)
Settings > Base de Datos tab loads and shows Hard Reset section
Hard Reset modal displays 'Contraseña Actual del Administrador' with warning that system will generate a new password
Entering wrong password + 'RESETEAR' triggers readable toast 'Contraseña incorrecta'
NO '[object Object]' text rendered in UI
NO 'Objects are not valid as a React child' errors in browser console
handleHardReset error handler correctly maps Pydantic detail arrays via err.msg
action_items: Full E2E of a real successful hard reset (correct password) was intentionally SKIPPED to avoid wiping the shared DB / rotating admin credentials mid-session. Consider running that flow in an isolated environment.
Soft Reset flow was not exercised in UI (visible on page but not clicked). Recommend a follow-up smoke test.
